annotations.description: Internal identifier for the person.
Number assigned to a person to identify the person uniquely. Doesn't depend on the person type such as employee, contingent worker, and so on.
Person's last name.

Person's display name.

Person's order name.
Person's work e-mail address.

Person's previous last name.
Person's military rank.

Finds all the workers based on the specified primary key attributes.
Unique Identifier for the worker.

Finds the assistant of the worker based on the specified attributes. The finder retrieves the worker assistant if the personEFF custom context Assistant is defined for the worker. You must create a custom context Assistant for person extensible flexfield where the PEI_INFORMATION_NUMBER1 attribute must be configured to capture the person identifier of the assistant.
Internal identifier for the person whose assistant is to be retrieved.
Number assigned to a person to identify the person uniquely. This is the number of the person whose assistant is to be retrieved.
Finds all the worker assignments based on the specified attributes.
Assignment name for the worker assignment.
Department name for the worker assignment.
Display name of the worker.
Job name for the worker assignment.
Country for the worker assignment.
Location name for the worker assignment.
Town or city for the worker assignment.
Indicates whether the finder attributes apply only on the primary assignment of the worker. The default value is false.
Person number of the worker.
Position name for the worker assignment.
Work email of the worker.
Finds all the worker managers based on the specified attributes.
Internal identifier for the assignment of worker whose managers are to be retrieved.
Unique identifier for the assignment of worker whose managers are to be retrieved.
Indicates whether the manager are retrieved only for line manager relationship. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Internal identifier for the person whose managers are to be retrieved.
Number assigned to a person to identify the person uniquely. This is the number of the person whose managers are to be retrieved.
Indicates whether the current assignment is the primary assignment for the worker whose managers are to be retrieved. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Type of managerial relationship with the report such as line manager.
Finds all the worker peers based on the specified attributes.
Internal identifier for the assignment of the worker whose peers are to be retrieved.
Unique identifier for the assignment of the worker whose peers are to be retrieved.
Indicates whether the peers are retrieved only for line manager relationship. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Internal identifier for the person whose peers are to be retrieved.
Number assigned to a person to identify the person uniquely. This is the number of the person whose peers are to be retrieved.
Indicates whether the current assignment is the primary assignment for the worker whose peers are to be retrieved. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Type of managerial relationship with the report such as line manager.
Finds all the worker reports based on the specified attributes.
Internal identifier for the assignment.
Unique identifier for the worker assignment.
Indicates whether only direct reports should be retrieved for the worker. Valid values are true or false. The default value is true.
Indicates whether the reports are retrieved only for line manager relationship. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Internal identifier for the person whose reports are to be retrieved.
Number assigned to a person to identify the person uniquely. This is the number of the person whose reports are to be retrieved.
Indicates whether the reports are retrieved for the primary assignment of the worker. Valid values are true or false. The default value is false.
Type of managerial relationship with the report such as line manager.

The publicWorkers resource provides all the active workers as of the current date and returns public information about workers. Worker types include employee, contingent worker, pending worker, and nonworker. For bulk extract operations, you must use HCM Extracts. Also, you must not use REST APIs for detecting data changes in the application. Instead, use Atom feeds to detect or identify changes made to data for key transactions in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM. For example, you may use Atom feeds to detect changes in employee's personal details, employment data, new hires, and terminations. See the Atom Feeds section for details about delivered feeds in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.
Internal identifier for the assignment.
Unique identifier for the worker assignment.
Name of the worker assignment.
Name of the legal employer for the assignment.
Start date of the period of employment.
Indicates whether the current assignment is the primary assignment for the worker. Valid values are Yes and No.
Indicates whether the current assignment is primary for the work relationship. Valid values are Yes and No.
Worker type for the period of service such as Employee (E), Contingent Worker (C), Pending Worker (P), or Nonworker (N).
Unique identifier for a worker.
Indicates whether the person is working at home. Valid values are Yes and No.
Indicates whether the worker is working full time or part time.
Name of the manager.

Name of the department for the worker assignment.
Code of the job for the worker assignment.

Code of the location for the worker assignment.
Name of the location for the worker assignment.
Code of the grade for the worker assignment.
Name of the grade for the worker assignment.
Building information associated with the work location.
Floor information associated with the work location.
Office number associated with the work location.
Internal mail location.

Primary region in which the work address is located.

Subregion within the secondary region.

Long postal code of the work location.

Single line address.

Length of service of a worker in additional months that are less than a full year.
Length of service of a worker in additional days that are less than a full month.
Indicates whether the current person is marked as favorite for a particular user. Valid values are Yes and No.
Finds all worker assignments based on the primary key attributes specified.
Unique identifier for the worker assignment.
Finds all worker assignments based on the attributes specified.
Unique Identifier for the worker assignment.
The assignments resource is a child of the publicWorkers resource. It includes all the active assignments of a worker as of the current date.

Name of the image file of the representative.
Unique location of the image. Encrypted version of the file.
Finds all worker reports based on the primary key attributes specified.
Internal identifier for the reports assignment.
Internal identifier for the worker photo.
Type of managerial relationship with the report.
The allReports resource is a child of the assignments resource. It includes all the reports of a worker as of the current date for a specific assignment.
Unique identifier for the department.
Name of the department.
Internal identifier for the department manager.

Name of the image file.
Count of workers in the department.
Finds all the departments of all reports based on the specified primary key attributes.
Unique identifier for the department.

Finds all the departments that all reports of a worker belong to, for the specified worker type and relationship type. The worker count and the departments returned with this resource must honor the finder criteria.
Finds departments of all reports of a worker that match the specified relationship type criteria.
Finds departments of all reports of a worker that match the specified worker type criteria.

The allReportsDepartments resource is a child of the assignments resource. It includes all the distinct departments that all the reports of a worker belong to, as of the system date for a specific assignment.
Surrogate identifier for the location.
Code of the location.
Name of the location.
Count of the workers in the location.
Finds all the locations of all reports based on the specified primary key attributes.

Finds all locations that all reports of a worker belong to, for the specified worker type and relationship type. The worker count and the locations returned with this resource must honor the finder criteria.
Finds locations of all reports of a worker that match the specified relationship type criteria.
Finds locations of all reports of a worker that match the specified worker type criteria.

The allReportsLocations resource is a child of the assignments resource. It includes all the distinct locations that all the reports of a worker belong to, as of the system date for a specific assignment.
